client.c(3) Library Functions Manual client.c(3)

NAME

client.c - ACIP client-side protocol implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t acip_client_receive_and_dispatch (acip_transport_t *transport, const acip_client_callbacks_t *callbacks)
asciichat_error_t acip_send_image_frame (acip_transport_t *transport, const void *pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height, uint32_t pixel_format)
asciichat_error_t acip_send_image_frame_h265 (acip_transport_t *transport, h265_encoder_t *encoder, const void *pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height)
Send H.265-encoded video frame to server. asciichat_error_t acip_send_client_join (acip_transport_t *transport, uint8_t capabilities)
asciichat_error_t acip_send_client_leave (acip_transport_t *transport)
asciichat_error_t acip_send_stream_start (acip_transport_t *transport, uint8_t stream_types)
asciichat_error_t acip_send_stream_stop (acip_transport_t *transport, uint8_t stream_types)
asciichat_error_t acip_send_capabilities (acip_transport_t *transport, const void *cap_data, size_t cap_len)
asciichat_error_t acip_send_protocol_version (acip_transport_t *transport, const protocol_version_packet_t *version)

Detailed Description

ACIP client-side protocol implementation.

Provides client-side ACIP protocol implementation for:

  • Session discovery and management
  • WebRTC signaling relay
  • String reservation (future)

ACIP (ascii-chat IP Protocol) is the wire protocol for session discovery and WebRTC signaling. ACDS is the reference server implementation.

Client-side packet sending and receiving for ascii-chat protocol. Handles client→server communication (image frames, join/leave, streaming control).

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Definition in file lib/network/acip/client.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t acip_client_receive_and_dispatch (acip_transport_t * transport, const acip_client_callbacks_t * callbacks)

Definition at line 30 of file lib/network/acip/client.c.

31                                                                                              {
32   if (!transport || !callbacks) {
33     log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ INVALID_PARAMS: transport=%p, callbacks=%p", (void *)transport, (void *)callbacks);
34     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport or callbacks");
35   }
36 
37   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 📥 RECV_START: transport=%p, checking connection state", (void *)transport);
38 
39   // Check if transport is connected
40   if (!transport->methods->is_connected(transport)) {
41     log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ NOT_CONNECTED: transport not ready for receive");
42     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Transport not connected");
43   }
44 
45   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] ✅ CONNECTED: transport ready, checking transport type");
46 
47   packet_envelope_t envelope;
48   bool enforce_encryption = (transport->crypto_ctx != NULL);
49   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 🔐 CRYPTO_MODE: enforce_encryption=%s", enforce_encryption ? "yes" : "no");
50 
51   // Try to get socket from transport
52   socket_t sock = transport->methods->get_socket(transport);
53   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 🔌 TRANSPORT_TYPE: socket=%d (socket=TCP, INVALID=WebRTC/other)", sock);
54 
55   if (sock != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
56     // Socket-based transport (TCP): use receive_packet_secure() for socket I/O + parsing
57     log_info("[ACIP_RECV] 🔌 TCP_PATH: using receive_packet_secure(), sockfd=%d", sock);
58     packet_recv_result_t result = receive_packet_secure(sock, transport->crypto_ctx, enforce_encryption, &envelope);
59 
60     log_info("[ACIP_RECV] 📥 TCP_RECV_RESULT: code=%d (0=success, 1=eof, 2=crypto_error, 3=other)", result);
61 
62     // Handle receive errors
63     if (result != PACKET_RECV_SUCCESS) {
64       if (result == PACKET_RECV_EOF) {
65         log_warn("[ACIP_RECV] ⚠️  EOF: Server closed connection");
66         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection closed (EOF)");
67       } else if (result == PACKET_RECV_SECURITY_VIOLATION) {
68         log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ SECURITY_VIOLATION: Encryption policy violated");
69         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Security violation: unencrypted packet when encryption required");
70       } else {
71         log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ TCP_RECV_FAILED: receive_packet_secure returned %d", result);
72         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive packet");
73       }
74     }
75   } else {
76     // Non-socket transport (WebRTC): use transport's recv() method to get complete packet
77     log_info("[ACIP_RECV] 🌐 WEBRTC_PATH: using transport->methods->recv()");
78     void *packet_data = NULL;
79     void *allocated_buffer = NULL;
80     size_t packet_len = 0;
81 
82     log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 🌐 WEBRTC_RECV: calling recv() method");
83     asciichat_error_t recv_result = transport->methods->recv(transport, &packet_data, &packet_len, &allocated_buffer);
84     log_info("[ACIP_RECV] 🌐 WEBRTC_RECV_RESULT: error=%d, packet_len=%zu, data=%p, alloc=%p", recv_result, packet_len,
85              packet_data, allocated_buffer);
86 
87     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
88       log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ WEBRTC_RECV_FAILED: error code %d", recv_result);
89       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Transport recv() failed");
90     }
91 
92     // Parse packet header
93     log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 🌐 HEADER_CHECK: packet_len=%zu, header_size=%zu", packet_len, sizeof(packet_header_t));
94     if (packet_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
95       log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ HEADER_TOO_SMALL: %zu < %zu", packet_len, sizeof(packet_header_t));
96       buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
97       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Packet too small: %zu < %zu", packet_len, sizeof(packet_header_t));
98     }
99 
100     const packet_header_t *header = (const packet_header_t *)packet_data;
101     envelope.type = NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
102     envelope.len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->length);
103     envelope.data = (uint8_t *)packet_data + sizeof(packet_header_t);
104     envelope.allocated_buffer = allocated_buffer;
105     envelope.allocated_size = packet_len;
106     envelope.was_encrypted = false; // WebRTC currently doesn't support encryption in this path
107 
108     log_info("[ACIP_RECV] 🌐 HEADER_PARSED: type=%u (0x%04x), len=%u, total_size=%zu", envelope.type, envelope.type,
109              envelope.len, packet_len);
110 
111     log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "WebRTC received packet: type=%u, len=%u, total_size=%zu", envelope.type,
112                   envelope.len, packet_len);
113 
114     // Validate packet length
115     log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 🌐 LENGTH_VALIDATION: payload_size=%u vs actual=%zu", envelope.len,
116               packet_len - sizeof(packet_header_t));
117     if (envelope.len != packet_len - sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
118       log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ LENGTH_MISMATCH: header=%u, actual=%zu", envelope.len,
119                 packet_len - sizeof(packet_header_t));
120       buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
121       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Packet length mismatch: header says %u, actual %zu", envelope.len,
122                        packet_len - sizeof(packet_header_t));
123     }
124   }
125 
126   // Handle PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED from server (decrypt and extract inner packet)
127   // Uses shared decryption logic with receive_packet_secure() for TCP
128   if (envelope.type == PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED) {
129     log_info("[ACIP_RECV] 🔐 ENCRYPTED_PACKET: decrypting PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED (len=%u)", envelope.len);
130     asciichat_error_t decrypt_result = packet_decrypt_envelope(&envelope, transport->crypto_ctx);
131     if (decrypt_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
132       log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ DECRYPT_FAILED: %d", decrypt_result);
133       buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
134       return decrypt_result;
135     }
136   }
137 
138   // Dispatch packet to appropriate ACIP handler
139   log_info("[ACIP_RECV] 🎯 DISPATCH_START: type=%u (0x%04x), data_len=%u, callbacks=%p", envelope.type, envelope.type,
140            envelope.len, (void *)callbacks);
141   asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
142       acip_handle_client_packet(transport, envelope.type, envelope.data, envelope.len, callbacks);
143 
144   if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
145     log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ DISPATCH_FAILED: type=%u, error=%d", envelope.type, dispatch_result);
146   } else {
147     log_info("[ACIP_RECV] ✅ DISPATCH_OK: type=%u (0x%04x) handled by callbacks", envelope.type, envelope.type);
148   }
149 
150   // Always free the allocated buffer (even if handler failed)
151   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 🗑️  CLEANUP: freeing buffer %p (size=%zu)", envelope.allocated_buffer,
152             envelope.allocated_size);
153   if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
154     buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
155   }
156 
157   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] ✅ RECV_COMPLETE: type=%u, result=%d", envelope.type,
158             dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? -1 : 0);
159 
160   // Return handler result
161   return dispatch_result;
162 }




References acip_handle_client_packet(),
    buffer_pool_free(), packet_decrypt_envelope(),
    receive_packet_secure(), and SET_ERRNO.

322   }

asciichat_error_t
  acip_send_image_frame (acip_transport_t * transport, const void *
  pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height, uint32_t pixel_format)


Definition at line 168 of file
    lib/network/acip/client.c.


169                                                                                 {
170   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: Called with %ux%u, transport=%p, pixel_data=%p", width, height, (void *)transport,
171             pixel_data);
172 
173   if (!transport || !pixel_data) {
174     log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: Invalid params");
175     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport or pixel_data");
176   }
177 
178   // Calculate pixel data size (3 bytes per pixel for RGB24)
179   size_t pixel_size = (size_t)width * height * 3;
180   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: pixel_size=%zu", pixel_size);
181 
182   // Create image frame packet header
183   image_frame_packet_t header;
184   header.width = HOST_TO_NET_U32(width);
185   header.height = HOST_TO_NET_U32(height);
186   header.pixel_format = HOST_TO_NET_U32(pixel_format);
187   header.compressed_size = 0;
188   header.checksum = 0;
189   header.timestamp = 0;
190 
191   // Calculate total size
192   size_t total_size;
193   if (checked_size_add(sizeof(header), pixel_size, &total_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
194     log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: Overflow in size calculation");
195     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Packet size overflow");
196   }
197 
198   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: total_size=%zu, allocating buffer", total_size);
199 
200   // Allocate buffer
201   uint8_t *buffer = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, total_size);
202   if (!buffer) {
203     log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: Failed to allocate %zu bytes", total_size);
204     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer: %zu bytes", total_size);
205   }
206 
207   // Build packet
208   memcpy(buffer, &header, sizeof(header));
209   memcpy(buffer + sizeof(header), pixel_data, pixel_size);
210 
211   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: About to send packet");
212   // Send via transport
213   asciichat_error_t result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_IMAGE_FRAME, buffer, total_size, 0);
214 
215   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: packet_send_via_transport returned %d", result);
216 
217   buffer_pool_free(NULL, buffer, total_size);
218   return result;
219 }






References buffer_pool_alloc(), buffer_pool_free(),
    packet_send_via_transport(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by threaded_send_image_frame().






asciichat_error_t
  acip_send_image_frame_h265 (acip_transport_t * transport,
  h265_encoder_t * encoder, const void * pixel_data, uint32_t width,
  uint32_t height)


Send H.265-encoded video frame to server. Encodes frame using
    H.265 codec and sends as PACKET_TYPE_IMAGE_FRAME_H265. Packet format:
    [flags:u8][width:u16][height:u16][h265_encoded_data...]




Parameters


transport ACIP transport


encoder H.265 encoder instance


pixel_data RGB pixel data from capture


width Frame width in pixels


height Frame height in pixels


Returns


Error code


Definition at line 234 of file
    lib/network/acip/client.c.


235                                                                                                       {
236   if (!transport || !encoder || !pixel_data) {
237     log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME_H265: Invalid params");
238     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport, encoder, or pixel_data");
239   }
240 
241   // Allocate output buffer for H.265 encoding
242   // H.265 can produce output larger than input due to codec overhead
243   // Use 2x multiplier to account for worst-case frame content
244   size_t output_buffer_size = 5 + (width * height * 2);
245   uint8_t *output_buffer = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, output_buffer_size);
246   if (!output_buffer) {
247     log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME_H265: Failed to allocate output buffer");
248     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate H.265 output buffer: %zu bytes", output_buffer_size);
249   }
250 
251   // Encode frame
252   size_t encoded_size = output_buffer_size - 5;
253   asciichat_error_t encode_result = h265_encode(encoder, (uint16_t)width, (uint16_t)height, (const uint8_t *)pixel_data,
254                                                 output_buffer + 5, &encoded_size);
255 
256   if (encode_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
257     log_error("H.265 encoding failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(encode_result));
258     buffer_pool_free(NULL, output_buffer, output_buffer_size);
259     return encode_result;
260   }
261 
262   // Prepare H.265 packet: [flags:u8][width:u16][height:u16][h265_data...]
263   output_buffer[0] = 0; // flags: no flags set by default
264   output_buffer[1] = (width >> 8) & 0xFF;
265   output_buffer[2] = width & 0xFF;
266   output_buffer[3] = (height >> 8) & 0xFF;
267   output_buffer[4] = height & 0xFF;
268 
269   size_t total_size = 5 + encoded_size;
270 
271   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME_H265: Encoded %ux%u → %zu bytes", width, height, total_size);
272 
273   // Send via transport
274   asciichat_error_t result =
275       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_IMAGE_FRAME_H265, output_buffer, total_size, 0);
276 
277   buffer_pool_free(NULL, output_buffer, output_buffer_size);
278   return result;
279 }






References buffer_pool_alloc(), buffer_pool_free(),
    h265_encode(), packet_send_via_transport(), and
    SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by threaded_send_image_frame_h265().

asciichat_error_t
  acip_send_stream_start (acip_transport_t * transport, uint8_t
  stream_types)


Definition at line 299 of file
    lib/network/acip/client.c.


299                                                                                             {
300   if (!transport) {
301     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport");
302   }
303 
304   // Server expects uint32_t (4 bytes), not uint8_t
305   uint32_t stream_types_net = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)stream_types);
306   return packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_STREAM_START, &stream_types_net, sizeof(stream_types_net), 0);
307 }






References packet_send_via_transport(), and
    SET_ERRNO.

313 
314   // Server expects uint32_t (4 bytes), not uint8_t
